expectations.text: Route parameter is named `{user}` (matching the entity type machine name) for automatic entity upcasting -- the controller does NOT manually call User::load() or \Drupal\user\Entity\User::load() (Drupal auto-loads when param name matches entity type)
expectations.evidence: user_activity.routing.yml line 2 uses path '/user/{user}/activity' and line 13 uses '/api/user/{user}/activity'. Grep of src/Controller/ for 'User::load' and '\Drupal\user\Entity\User::load' returned no matches. Both controller methods receive a typed UserInterface parameter, confirming auto-upcasting is used.

expectations.text: Route file defines `_title_callback` pointing to a controller method for dynamic page title (not just a static `_title` string -- dynamic titles need a callback that receives the upcasted entity)
expectations.evidence: user_activity.routing.yml line 5: '_title_callback: '\Drupal\user_activity\Controller\UserActivityController::getTitle''. The corresponding method at UserActivityController.php line 74 accepts a UserInterface parameter and returns a dynamic string using the user's display name.

expectations.text: The custom service class does NOT extend ControllerBase -- services are standalone classes with constructor injection via .services.yml arguments (not the create() factory pattern which is for controllers)
expectations.evidence: UserActivityService.php line 12: 'class UserActivityService {' — no extends clause. Constructor at line 36 accepts Connection and EntityTypeManagerInterface. user_activity.services.yml lines 3-6 wire it with arguments '@database' and '@entity_type.manager'. No create() factory method present in the service.

expectations.text: Controller does NOT use static \Drupal::service() or \Drupal::entityTypeManager() calls -- injects all dependencies via create() + constructor (static calls bypass DI, making code untestable)
expectations.evidence: Grep of src/Controller/ for '\Drupal::' returned no matches. Both UserActivityController.php and UserActivityApiController.php use create() + constructor injection only. The static \Drupal::service() call at line 59 is in UserActivityService.php (the service), not in any controller — the expectation explicitly scopes to the Controller.

expectations.text: Routes use `_permission` for access control (not `_access: 'TRUE'` which bypasses all access checking including for anonymous users)
expectations.evidence: user_activity.routing.yml lines 7 and 17 both use '_entity_access: "user.view"' — not '_permission'. The expectation specifically requires '_permission' as the access mechanism. '_entity_access' is a different access key and does not satisfy this requirement.

expectations.text: The module enables successfully: ddev drush en user_activity -y returns exit code 0
expectations.evidence: Command output: '[notice] Already installed: user_activity' with exit code 0, confirming successful enable.

expectations.text: Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /user/1/activity -- verify the page renders with a dynamic title containing the admin user's display name and shows activity summary content (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: eval-browser report: page rendered with title 'Activity for admin | Drush Site-Install', content shows 'Nodes Authored: 0', 'Last Access: 03/07/2026 - 07:36'. HTTP 200.

expectations.text: Navigate to /api/user/1/activity -- verify the response contains valid JSON with activity data fields (via eval-browser)
expectations.evidence: eval-browser report: API returned valid JSON with fields uid=1, username=admin, nodes_authored=0, last_access=1772894210. HTTP 200.

expectations.text: Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
expectations.evidence: phpcs result shows 0 ERRORS and 1 WARNING (line 59 of UserActivityService.php: '\Drupal calls should be avoided in classes'). Warnings do not constitute failures — the expectation asks for no errors, and the provided result confirms 0 errors.
